description:
Add continuous point-in-time recovery (PITR) and first-class forking to the SQLite v2 storage layer. Branches become the unit of identity; bookmarks (33-char lex-sortable strings) name commits; `fork()` and `restore_to_bookmark()` are metadata-only at the storage layer (copy-on-write at the layer level). FoundationDB stays the hot tier. S3 (or filesystem in dev) is the cold tier holding LTX V3 layer files for history. A new cold compactor service drains DELTAs into immutable layers, maintains a sparse BookmarkIndex, and runs dependency-graph GC against a 30-day retention window. Both compactors are UPS-driven (no cron). The spec lives at `.agent/specs/sqlite-rough-pitr.md`. Read it first.

===== KEY INVARIANTS =====

- Branches are the unit of identity. An actor's storage = the chain of branches reachable from its head by parent pointers. New keys: `[BRANCHES]/head`, `[BRANCHES]/list/{id}`, `[BR][branch_id]/...` for per-branch storage.
- Bookmarks are 33 chars: `{timestamp_ms_hex_be:016}-{txid_hex_be:016}`. Branch identity is NOT in the wire format; bookmarks are interpreted relative to a branch context. Lex order = chronological within a single branch's parent chain only; siblings break global lex order.
- Fork is metadata-only: regular-read parent's `retention_pin_txid` (OCC fence), depth check (cap MAX_FORK_DEPTH=16), allocate Uuid, write Branch, atomic-min `oldest_descendant_parent_txid`, atomic-add refcount. No data copy. `restore_to_bookmark` is the same primitive; pegboard decides whether to swap head.
- FoundationDB is hot tier. S3 is cold tier (`ColdTier::Disabled` for local dev, `ColdTier::Filesystem` for tests, `ColdTier::S3` for prod, `ColdTier::Faulty` wrapper for fault injection).
- Rolling checksum lives on `/META/head` as `post_apply_checksum: u64` (CRC-ISO-64 XOR fold). Hot-path commit RTT count is unchanged from stateless spec (1).
- `COMMITS/{T}` value is just `i64 LE wall_clock_ms` (no checksum); checksum is on `/META/head` (rolling) and per-layer LTX trailers (cold).
- Cold compactor is UPS-driven (NO periodic cron). Drain trigger fires when hot compactor's pass advances `materialized_txid` past `cold_compact_delta_threshold` (1024 deltas) beyond `cold_drained_txid`. Fork warmup is its own UPS payload. GC self-publishes at end of drain pass.
- Cold pass is Phase A (FDB read tx, <2s) → Phase B (S3-only, no FDB tx) → Phase C (FDB write tx, <3s with OCC fence on `cold_drained_txid`). Phase B uses HWM `pending/{uuid}.marker` files for orphan reconciliation.
- Layer filenames `delta/{min_txid}-{max_txid}.ltx` OMIT checksum so re-uploads are idempotent at the S3 key level.
- GC runs as Phase C-2 holding the cold lease. Pin = min(time_floor_txid, oldest_descendant_parent_txid). NO monotonic ratchet. Frozen branches use creation-relative wall-clock retention, not head-relative.
- Branch deletion cascade: refcount=0 check → state=Tombstoned → clear hot prefix synchronously → cold compactor's next pass walks S3 prefix → state=Deleted. Actor-destroy uses a gasoline workflow for batch DeleteObjects.
- Read path on a fork descendant uses flattened ancestry (ancestors[] vec, capped at MAX_FORK_DEPTH=16, with lazy PIDX cache per ancestor). Worst case 16 ancestors × 2 in-tx gets per pgno on cold cache; mitigated by lazy cache + fork warmup that pre-stages parent image layers in the descendant.
- Burst mode: when cold-pod 5xx ratio exceeds threshold over 3 passes, hot quota cap raises from 10 GiB to 20 GiB (HOT_BURST_MULTIPLIER=2). Reverts when cold tier recovers.
- Billable cold bytes per branch = sum of layer files OWNED by this branch (created by its own cold passes, including fork-warmup-copied layers). Fork descendants start at 0 billable cold bytes.
- All cross-pod-persisted S3 objects (`ColdManifest`, `BranchColdState`, `BookmarkIndex`) carry `schema_version: u32` via vbare. The cold compactor reads old version + writes new version on every pass; reader retains prior-version code paths for one full retention window past any version bump.
- Breaking changes are unconditionally acceptable. The system has not shipped to production. Wire shape, on-disk key layout, and existing schemas are all free to change.

===== ARCHITECTURAL CONTEXT =====

- All work is in `engine/packages/sqlite-storage/`. Spec is `.agent/specs/sqlite-rough-pitr.md`.
- New top-level modules: `pump/branches.rs`, `pump/bookmark.rs`, `pump/checksum.rs`, `pump/ancestry.rs`, `cold_compactor/`, `cold_tier/`, `burst_mode.rs`.
- Cold compactor is a new `ServiceKind::Standalone` service registered in `engine/packages/engine/src/run_config.rs` next to the hot compactor.
- Stateless spec (`sqlite-storage-stateless.md`) is the prerequisite; this work extends it. Hot compactor, ActorDb, META split, lease pattern, UPS triggers, atomic-add quota — all already exist and stay in place.
- All tests live under `tests/` (no inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `src/`).
- Use `vbare::OwnedVersionedData` + versioned `*.bare` schema for all persisted/wire formats. Raw `serde_bare` only for ephemeral in-memory encodings.
- Use `scc::HashMap` / `moka::Cache` / `DashMap` for concurrent maps; never `Mutex<HashMap>`. Use `tokio::sync::Mutex` / `RwLock` in async code; `parking_lot` only in forced-sync contexts.
- Errors: `anyhow` from failable functions, `RivetError` derive for typed errors, no glob imports from anyhow, prefer `.context()` over `anyhow!`.
- Logs: `tracing::info!(?x, "foo")` with structured fields; lowercase messages; no `println!`/`eprintln!`.

===== RUN COMMANDS =====

From repo root:

- Compile-check a crate: `cargo check -p <package-name>` (preferred for verification).
- Build a crate: `cargo build -p <package-name>`.
- Run all tests for a crate: `cargo test -p <package-name>`.
- Run a single test file: `cargo test -p sqlite-storage --test <file_stem>`.
- Run a single test: `cargo test -p <package-name> <test_name> -- --nocapture`.
- Never run `cargo fmt` or `./scripts/cargo/fix.sh` — the team formats at merge time.

===== READ BEFORE STARTING =====

- `.agent/specs/sqlite-rough-pitr.md` — the full v2 spec this PRD implements.
- `.agent/specs/sqlite-storage-stateless.md` — the prerequisite stateless spec.
- `.agent/research/review-architecture.md`, `review-performance.md`, `review-operations.md` — adversarial reviews that shaped v2.
- `engine/CLAUDE.md` — VBARE migration rules, Epoxy keys, SQLite storage tests.
- `CLAUDE.md` at repo root — layer constraints, fail-by-default rules, async lock rules, error handling.
- `docs-internal/engine/sqlite-storage.md` — current SQLite storage crash course.
- `docs-internal/engine/sqlite-vfs.md` — VFS parity rules.
- `engine/packages/sqlite-storage/src/` — current crate (already stateless).
- `engine/packages/sqlite-storage/src/compactor/` — reference pattern for the cold compactor (lease, UPS subscriber, pass orchestration).

userStories.title: Add new identifier types and branch records to pump/types.rs
userStories.description: Introduce the new identifier types (NamespaceId, NamespacePointerId, ActorPointerId, NamespaceBranchId, ActorBranchId) and the immutable branch records (NamespaceBranchRecord, ActorBranchRecord, NamespacePointer, ActorPointer) with vbare-compatible derives in pump/types.rs. Also add Tier and BranchState (with Live and Frozen variants) enums. Do not yet wire them into commit/read paths.
acceptanceCriteria: NamespaceId, NamespacePointerId, ActorPointerId, NamespaceBranchId, ActorBranchId types are defined matching spec section 5.1
ActorBranchRecord and NamespaceBranchRecord structs match spec section 5.3 and 5.4 exactly (parent, parent_versionstamp, root_versionstamp, fork_depth, created_at_ms, created_from_bookmark, state)
ActorPointer and NamespacePointer carry current_branch and last_swapped_at_ms
Tier enum has T0/T1/T2 variants; BranchState has Live and Frozen variants
All structs derive Encode/Decode via vbare conventions used elsewhere in the crate
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add namespace tier state, pin status, resolved versionstamp, and branch manifest types
userStories.description: Add NamespaceTierState, PinStatus (Pending|Ready|Failed), ResolvedVersionstamp, BranchManifest, BookmarkRef, and BookmarkStr to pump/types.rs. These are referenced by operations and commit paths added later.
acceptanceCriteria: NamespaceTierState carries tier and promoted_at_versionstamp
PinStatus enum has Pending/Ready/Failed
ResolvedVersionstamp carries versionstamp + Option<BookmarkRef>
BranchManifest carries cold_drained_txid, last_hot_pass_txid, last_access_ts_ms, last_access_bucket per spec section 6
BookmarkRef carries bookmark + Option<resolved_versionstamp>; BookmarkStr is length-checked at construction
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cold-tier and pointer-snapshot data types
userStories.description: Add ColdManifestIndex, ColdManifestChunkRef, ColdManifestChunk, LayerEntry, LayerKind, BookmarkIndexEntry, PointerSnapshot, BookmarkRecord, PinnedBookmarkRecord, CommitRow (extended), DBHead (extended) types matching spec sections 6 and 7. Each persisted S3 type carries schema_version: u32.
acceptanceCriteria: DBHead extended with post_apply_checksum and branch_id fields
CommitRow carries wall_clock_ms, versionstamp, db_size_pages, post_apply_checksum
ColdManifestIndex/ColdManifestChunk/LayerEntry/BookmarkIndexEntry/PointerSnapshot types match spec section 7
BookmarkRecord and PinnedBookmarkRecord defined for FDB BOOKMARK partition
All persisted S3-bound types carry schema_version: u32
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add new FDB key-builder partitions in pump/keys.rs
userStories.description: Add FDB key builders for the new partitions: APTR (0x10), NSPTR (0x11), BRANCHES (0x20), NSBRANCH (0x21), CTR (0x40), BOOKMARK (0x50), CMPC (0x60), and update BR (0x30) prefix to live under `BR/{actor_branch_id}/`. Include /cur, /history/{ts:8}{nonce:4}, /refcount, /desc_pin, /bk_pin, /tier_state, /actor_tombstones, /eviction_index sub-keys per spec section 6. Also add VTX/{versionstamp_be:16} and META/head_at_fork.
acceptanceCriteria: APTR key builders for cur and history/{ts_ms_be:8}{nonce_be:4}, keyed by (namespace_branch_id, actor_id)
NSPTR key builders for cur and history
BRANCHES list/{actor_branch_id}/ with refcount, desc_pin, bk_pin sub-keys
NSBRANCH list/{ns_branch_id}/ with refcount, desc_pin, bk_pin, tier_state, actor_tombstones/{actor_id} sub-keys

BR/{actor_branch_id}/ keys for META/head, META/head_at_fork, META/compact, META/cold_compact, META/quota, META/compactor_lease, META/cold_lease, META/manifest/{cold_drained_txid|last_hot_pass_txid|last_access_ts_ms|last_access_bucket}, COMMITS/{txid_be:8}, VTX/{versionstamp_be:16}, PIDX/{pgno_be:4}, DELTA/{txid_be:8}/{chunk_be:4}, SHARD/{shard_id_be:4}/{as_of_txid_be:8}
CTR/quota_global, CTR/eviction_index/{bucket_be:8}/{actor_branch_id}, BOOKMARK/{actor_id}/{bookmark_str}, CMPC/enqueue, CMPC/lease_global keys
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add tunable constants for PITR + forking
userStories.description: Add the public constants used throughout the crate: MAX_FORK_DEPTH=16, MAX_NAMESPACE_DEPTH=16, MAX_PINS_PER_NAMESPACE=1024, MAX_SHARD_VERSIONS_PER_SHARD=32, COMMITS_TIER0_RETENTION_MS=7 days, ACCESS_TOUCH_THROTTLE_MS=60_000, SHARD_RETENTION_MARGIN=64, FROZEN_BRANCH_RETENTION_MS, HOT_CACHE_WINDOW_MS, STALE_MARKER_AGE_MS, HOT_BURST_MULTIPLIER=2. Place in a single constants module.
acceptanceCriteria: All constants from spec section 12.1 defined with the documented defaults
Constants live in a single dedicated module (e.g. pump/constants.rs) and are pub use at crate root
Documentation comments cite the spec rationale for each constant
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add error variants for PITR + fork operations
userStories.description: Extend the SqliteStorageError enum with ForkChainTooDeep, NamespaceForkChainTooDeep, ForkOutOfRetention, BookmarkExpired, BranchNotReachable, ShardVersionCapExhausted, TooManyPins, ActorNotFound, and any other variants referenced by the spec's operation pseudocode.
acceptanceCriteria: All error variants referenced in spec sections 8, 9, 12 added
Errors integrate with existing RivetError derive pattern in the crate
Each variant carries a meaningful message with structured fields where useful
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate commit path to versioned SHARD writes
userStories.description: Update the hot compactor commit path so SHARDs are written under SHARD/{shard_id_be:4}/{as_of_txid_be:8} instead of overwriting a single SHARD per shard_id. Reads still take the largest as_of_txid <= read_txid. Ensure existing tests still pass with single-version-per-shard behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Hot compactor writes SHARD/{shard_id}/{max_folded_txid} keys instead of overwrites
SHARD reads do reverse-range scan on SHARD/{shard_id}/ constrained by as_of_txid <= V
Hot compactor records max_folded_txid in BranchManifest.last_hot_pass_txid sub-key
Existing single-branch tests pass without behavior change
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Write VTX index entry on every commit
userStories.description: Extend pump/commit.rs to write `VTX/{versionstamp_be:16} = u64 BE txid` via SetVersionstampedKey on each commit alongside COMMITS. CommitRow now carries the captured versionstamp + db_size_pages + post_apply_checksum. No extra RTT (folds into existing tx batch).
acceptanceCriteria: Every commit writes a VTX/{versionstamp:16} entry mapping versionstamp -> txid
VTX writes use SetVersionstampedKey so they share the same versionstamp as the COMMITS row
CommitRow includes wall_clock_ms, versionstamp, db_size_pages, post_apply_checksum
Test verifies VTX[v] -> txid lookup matches the txid of the commit with versionstamp v
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Allocate root branch on first actor commit and migrate META under BR/{branch_id}/
userStories.description: Update ActorDb so that on first commit it allocates a root ActorBranchId, writes the ActorBranchRecord to BRANCHES/list/{...}, writes the APTR/{ns_branch_id}/{actor_id}/cur pointer, and stores all subsequent META/COMMITS/PIDX/DELTA/SHARD under BR/{branch_id}/ instead of the legacy per-actor prefix. Existing tests with a single-branch actor must still pass.
acceptanceCriteria: First commit on an actor allocates a root ActorBranchRecord and writes APTR + BRANCHES
All META/COMMITS/PIDX/DELTA/SHARD writes for that actor land under BR/{branch_id}/
ActorDb caches the resolved branch_id on its in-memory struct
Existing single-branch crate tests pass unmodified
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Allocate root namespace branch and write NSPTR on namespace creation
userStories.description: Wire namespace creation so that creating a namespace allocates a root NamespaceBranchId, writes NamespaceBranchRecord under NSBRANCH/list/{...}, writes the NamespacePointer under NSPTR/{ns_id}/cur, and seeds the NamespaceTierState at T0. Adds the namespace branch_id to ActorBranchRecord on actor creation.
acceptanceCriteria: Namespace creation writes a NamespaceBranchRecord with parent=None, root_versionstamp set
NamespacePointer.cur points at the new branch with last_swapped_at_ms
NamespaceTierState is initialized at T0
Actor creation in that namespace records namespace_branch on ActorBranchRecord
Test verifies a fresh namespace has NSPTR + NSBRANCH + tier_state populated
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement derive_branch_at primitive
userStories.description: Implement the shared derive_branch_at primitive (spec section 8) that snapshots source's /META/head into the new branch's /META/head_at_fork via VTX + COMMITS lookup, OCC-fences on bk_pin, allocates the new branch record, atomic-mins desc_pin on the source, and atomic-adds refcounts. Used by both fork_actor and rollback_actor. Enforces MAX_FORK_DEPTH.
acceptanceCriteria: derive_branch_at(tx, source, at_versionstamp, new_id, ns_branch, bookmark_ref) implemented per spec section 8
OCC fence reads source's bk_pin and aborts with ForkOutOfRetention if pin > at_versionstamp
head_at_fork is reconstructed via VTX[v] -> txid then COMMITS[txid] read
Refcounts atomic-add on both source (descendant ref) and new branch (own pointer ref)
Source desc_pin atomic-min'd to at_versionstamp
MAX_FORK_DEPTH enforcement returns ForkChainTooDeep
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement derive_namespace_branch_at primitive
userStories.description: Implement the namespace twin of derive_branch_at: snapshots source NS branch parent_versionstamp, OCC-fences bk_pin, enforces MAX_NAMESPACE_DEPTH, allocates a NamespaceBranchRecord, atomic-mins desc_pin and adds refcounts. Used by both fork_namespace and rollback_namespace.
acceptanceCriteria: derive_namespace_branch_at implemented matching spec section 8.1 fork_namespace structure
MAX_NAMESPACE_DEPTH enforcement returns NamespaceForkChainTooDeep
OCC fence on source NSBRANCH bk_pin
Refcount atomic-add on source and new ns branch
Tier state inheritance left lazy via ensure_tier_at_least CAS
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ensure_tier_at_least CAS helper for T0 -> T1 promotion
userStories.description: Implement ensure_tier_at_least(tx, ns_branch_id, target_tier) which reads the namespace branch's tier_state mutable key and CAS-promotes T0 -> T1 atomically if needed. First fork or pinned bookmark on a namespace triggers the promotion.
acceptanceCriteria: ensure_tier_at_least reads NSBRANCH/list/{nb}/tier_state and CAS-promotes if below target
Promotion writes NamespaceTierState with tier=T1 and promoted_at_versionstamp
Concurrent promotion races resolve to a single winner; losers re-read T1
Test verifies first fork on a T0 namespace promotes to T1 atomically
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement fork_actor operation
userStories.description: Implement fork_actor(source_namespace, source_actor_id, at, target_namespace) per spec section 8.2. Resolves source APTR via ns branch chain, calls derive_branch_at, allocates new actor id, writes APTR for target namespace branch.
acceptanceCriteria: fork_actor returns a fresh ActorId with a new ActorBranchId
APTR/{target_ns_branch}/{new_actor_id}/cur points at the new branch
Source actor's data is reachable through the new branch's parent chain
Cross-namespace fork is supported (target_namespace != source_namespace)
Test forks at root, depth-1, and depth-N source branches
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement fork_namespace operation
userStories.description: Implement fork_namespace(source_ns_id, at) per spec section 8.1. Allocates new NamespaceId + NamespaceBranchId, writes NSPTR + NSBRANCH, atomic-min desc_pin on source, refcount adds. Does NOT eagerly materialize APTR entries; lazy resolution handled in a later story.
acceptanceCriteria: fork_namespace returns a fresh NamespaceId with a new NamespaceBranchId
NSPTR/{new_ns_id}/cur points at the new branch with parent_versionstamp set
Source NS branch desc_pin atomic-min'd; refcount incremented
MAX_NAMESPACE_DEPTH enforcement
No APTR keys written eagerly under the new ns branch
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement resolve_actor_pointer with lazy ns-branch parent walk
userStories.description: Implement resolve_actor_pointer(tx, ns_branch_id, actor_id) which tries APTR/{ns_branch_id}/{actor_id}/cur first, then walks the namespace branch parent chain capped by parent_versionstamp on miss. Honor actor_tombstones (return ActorNotFound if any tombstone is present in the chain between cur and the first hit).
acceptanceCriteria: Direct APTR hit returns immediately
Miss falls through to ns branch parent chain via NSBRANCH parent links
Tombstone in any intermediate ns branch returns ActorNotFound
Walk is capped by MAX_NAMESPACE_DEPTH
Test verifies lazy resolution after fork_namespace without re-materialization
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement rollback_actor operation with branch freezing
userStories.description: Implement rollback_actor(namespace, actor_id, at) per spec section 8.4. Calls derive_branch_at, freezes the previous branch (BranchState::Frozen), writes APTR history row with nonce, decrements old branch refcount, swaps APTR pointer.
acceptanceCriteria: rollback_actor allocates a new ActorBranchId at the rollback point
Previous branch transitions to BranchState::Frozen
APTR history row written under APTR/{ns_branch}/{actor_id}/history/{ts:8}{nonce:4}
APTR/cur swapped to new branch; old branch refcount atomic-add(-1)
Subsequent commit on the actor lands on the new branch's /META/head

userStories.description: Implement resolve_bookmark(namespace, actor_id, bookmark) per spec section 9. Bridges both actor branch parent chain AND namespace branch parent chain. Uses VTX[bookmark_versionstamp] -> txid for O(1) lookup. Returns ResolvedVersionstamp + bookmark ref or BookmarkExpired/BranchNotReachable.

NS-branch cap: walk parents until parent_versionstamp_i <= bookmark.versionstamp

Pinned bookmark hit (exact ts/txid match) returns its stored versionstamp + bookmark ref

Returns the bookmark string immediately.

userStories.title: Implement flattened ancestry cache on ActorDb

Built once per ActorDb lifetime by walking BRANCHES/list/{...}.parent links

userStories.title: Implement cache invalidation contract on commit and get_pages
userStories.description: Extend commit and get_pages to read APTR.last_swapped_at_ms and NSPTR.last_swapped_at_ms in parallel with /META/head via try_join! per spec section 10. On mismatch with cached values, the tx aborts; the caller drops the ancestry cache and re-resolves before retrying.
acceptanceCriteria: Every commit and get_pages reads APTR + NSPTR last_swapped_at_ms in parallel with /META/head
Mismatch aborts the tx and drops the ancestry cache
Caller re-resolves and retries automatically

userStories.title: Implement get_pages walking ancestry chain with PIDX/SHARD fallthrough
userStories.description: Update get_pages to walk the cached ancestry chain. For each pgno, walk current_branch -> root, doing PIDX read capped by parent_versionstamp on each ancestor; on PIDX miss fall through to versioned SHARD read with as_of_txid <= cap. Return zero page if exhausted.

userStories.title: Implement eviction predicate (3-gate: hot window + descendant pin + bookmark pin)
userStories.description: Implement the eviction predicate per spec section 12.3. Computes evictability via three gates: (1) older than HOT_CACHE_WINDOW_MS, (2) cold_drained_txid >= max_folded_txid, (3) no descendant pin (desc_pin > as_of_txid) and no bookmark pin (bk_pin > as_of_txid). Also enforces last_hot_pass_txid - SHARD_RETENTION_MARGIN >= as_of_txid.
acceptanceCriteria: Predicate returns true only when all three gates pass + hot pass margin satisfied
